perturbation.original_text: 7.1.1. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall start at the Effective Date and shall automatically terminate when the Project is terminated or completed.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1.1. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall start at the Effective Date and shall automatically terminate five years after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the termination condition of the agreement from project completion to a fixed duration of five years after the Effective Date. This contradicts section 7.3.1, which states that the obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure, and non-use survive termination, potentially indefinitely for trade secrets. With the modified text, it's unclear whether these surviving obligations still apply after the five-year fixed term, creating ambiguity and disputes about the long-term protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 5 (five) years from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for obligations to terminate upon project completion, aligning with the contradicted text (7.3.1) that some obligations survive termination. The changed text sets a fixed 5-year termination, creating a contradiction. If the project completes in 3 years, but obligations in 7.3.1 are meant to survive 'termination', it's unclear if those obligations continue for only 2 more years (to reach 5 from the effective date) or according to their own terms in 7.3.1 (potentially indefinitely for trade secrets). This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict, qualifying as an inconsistency-based in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloserȇs request, Recipient shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days from such request, return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof. Alternatively, at Discloserȇs option and request, Recipient shall destroy and/or erase all such materials and documentation and shall provide a written certification that all such materials and documentation have been destroyed and/or erased.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall make commercially reasonable efforts to return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof. Alternatively, at Discloser’s option and request, Recipient may destroy and/or erase all such materials and documentation and may provide a written certification that all such materials and documentation have been destroyed and/or erased.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text weakens the obligation to return confidential information by changing "shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days" to "shall make commercially reasonable efforts" and replacing "shall destroy and/or erase" with "may destroy and/or erase." This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the intention of section 8.1.2, which provides exceptions for information that must be stored by the Recipient by law or routine backup, with the implication that the general rule is a strict return or destruction requirement. Now, it is unclear under what circumstances the Recipient can claim commercial unreasonableness or simply opt not to destroy the information, leading to potential disputes over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1.2. Clause 8.1 shall not apply to Confidential Information which i) must be stored by Recipient according to provisions of mandatory law or ii) was made as a matter of routine backup provided that such Confidential Information and copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidential obligation according to the terms and conditions set forth herein until returned and/or destroyed, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information by changing the mandatory "shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days" to the discretionary "shall make commercially reasonable efforts" and "shall destroy and/or erase" to "may destroy and/or erase". This introduces uncertainty regarding the return or destruction of confidential information. This directly contradicts section 8.1.2, which provides exceptions only for information that *must* be stored by law or routine backup and implies strict compliance with section 8.1 by stating such information shall be subject to indefinite confidentiality until returned or destroyed. By introducing ambiguity and discretion into the destruction/return obligation, the modified 8.1.1 creates a strong contradiction with 8.1.2 and makes it unclear under what circumstances the Recipient can claim commercial unreasonableness or simply opt not to destroy the information, leading to potential disputes over compliance.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1.1. This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations for any of the Parties to this Agreement, to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project, and any Party can cease to continue such discussions at any time. The Agreement does not grant Recipient any exclusivity, and Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1.1. This Agreement shall constitute a commitment to negotiate in good faith for future projects with the Recipient for a period of one year. The Agreement grants Recipient exclusivity for similar projects during the term of this agreement, and Discloser and its Affiliates will not purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers without first offering the opportunity to the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text directly contradicts the original intent of Section 9.1 by establishing a commitment to negotiate future projects in good faith and granting the Recipient exclusivity for similar projects. This conflicts with the original clause stating that the agreement does not create any obligation to continue discussions or enter into further contracts. Specifically, it directly contradicts the statement that the Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services from other suppliers. The in-text contradiction now presents a conflict over whether the Discloser is obligated to work exclusively with the recipient, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall constitute a commitment to negotiate in good faith for future projects with the Recipient for a period of one year. The Agreement grants Recipient exclusivity for similar projects during the term of this agreement, and Discloser and its Affiliates will not purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers without first offering the opportunity to the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text establishes a commitment to negotiate future projects in good faith and grants the Recipient exclusivity, which directly contradicts the original clause stating that the agreement does not create any obligation to continue discussions or enter into further contracts and that Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services from other suppliers. The contradicted text and changed text are identical, but the explanation clearly demonstrates that the change causes a contradiction as the original text stated that the agreement shall not constitute any rights, while the changed text states that it constitutes a commitment and grants exclusivity, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2.2. Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach, termination or invalidity hereof which is not resolved by prior negotiations, shall be finally settled by arbitration by The Danish Institute of Arbitration in accordance with the relevant rules adapted by The Danish Institute of Arbitration and applicable at the time of opening of the arbitration. The arbitration tribunal must have three arbitrators appointed by the Danish Institute of Arbitration unless the Parties agree otherwise. The arbitrators elect, from among their members, the chairman of the arbitration tribunal.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2.2. Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach, termination or invalidity hereof which is not resolved by prior negotiations, shall be resolved in the ordinary courts of Denmark. Each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of such courts.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the mandatory arbitration clause and replaces it with a clause requiring disputes to be resolved in the ordinary courts of Denmark. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 10.2.4, which states that breach of the agreement may be stopped by injunctive relief at the ordinary courts, implying that arbitration is the primary means of dispute resolution while injunctive relief is a supplementary option. Now it is unclear if ordinary courts serve as a primary venue for all issues or are only for specific injunctive relief requests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2.4. Notwithstanding this Clause 10, breach of this Agreement may be stopped by injunctive relief at the ordinary courts. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as prohibiting Discloser from pursuing any remedies available in addition to those remedies available under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates arbitration for dispute resolution, while the changed text mandates resolution in the ordinary courts of Denmark. Section 10.2.4 states that breach of the agreement may be stopped by injunctive relief at the ordinary courts, implying that arbitration is the primary means of dispute resolution while injunctive relief is a supplementary option. The change removes the primary method of dispute resolution of arbitration, creating a strong contradiction with the text from section 10.2.4, making it unclear whether disputes will be handled primarily through the courts, arbitration, or both. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the different sections of the contract (10.2.2 and 10.2.4) provide conflicting venues for dispute resolution.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipientȇs work in relation to the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person, including Representatives, without explicit prior written approval from the Discloser for each instance of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text restricts the distribution of confidential information even to Representatives, requiring explicit prior written approval for each instance. This creates an in-text contradiction to Section 6.1, which states that Representatives shall be subject to the same confidentiality and restricted use obligations as the Recipient. Now the text is conflicting as the representatives have the same confidentiality obligations as the recipient (making them able to view confidential info), but at the same time, they cannot view it without explicit permission, creating uncertainty about the role and rights of representatives concerning confidential information and creating potential disputes and hurdles.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1. If Recipient discloses or distributes Confidential Information to any of its Representatives, such Representatives shall be subject to the same confidentiality and restricted use obligations as Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.1.3 now requires explicit prior written approval from the Discloser for each instance of disclosure of Confidential Information, even to Representatives. This directly contradicts Section 6.1, which states that Representatives shall be subject to the same confidentiality and restricted use obligations as the Recipient. The original text allowed Representatives to view confidential information to perform their work, while the modified text prohibits this without explicit permission, creating a clear inconsistency regarding Representatives' access to confidential information.
